decisions.title: Client-side parsing strategy for Explore V2
decisions.context: 4 tickets (ENG-9966-9969) implementing CSV, Parquet, HDF5, CH10 parsing client-side. Major architectural shift from server-side processing. Impacts Explore V2 performance, scalability, and 'Nail the Core' pillar.

arguments.for: Reduces server load, faster preview for users, enables offline capabilities, aligns with edge computing vision (Sift Edge)
arguments.against: Browser memory limits for large files, inconsistent performance across devices, security concerns with client-side parsing libraries, maintenance burden

decisions.title: SCIM enterprise rollout readiness
decisions.context: 3 tickets (ENG-9784, 9785, 9926) hardening SCIM: block admin deactivation, block email invites, add health visibility. Signals enterprise readiness for 'Embed Deeper' but may delay if not prioritized.

arguments.for: Enterprise customers require SSO/SCIM for compliance, removes friction for IT admins, increases stickiness, directly supports 'Embed Deeper' pillar
arguments.against: Complex edge cases in user provisioning, requires comprehensive testing, support burden if health monitoring insufficient

decisions.title: Service worker architecture expansion
decisions.context: ENG-9963 splitting GeoMaps and ScatterPlot into separate service workers. Part of Canvas modernization (ENG-9571 toolbar rework, ENG-9895 status updates). Pattern for 'Build the Moat' through performance.

arguments.for: Isolates chart rendering failures, enables parallel processing, improves Canvas responsiveness, sets pattern for other visualizations
arguments.against: Increased complexity in debugging, memory overhead from multiple workers, coordination challenges for shared data

decisions.title: IdP customer onboarding automation priority
decisions.context: ENG-9613 building internal tool for IdP onboarding, ENG-9614 creating metrics/alerts. New customer type requiring process automation. Impacts 'Embed Deeper' TAM expansion.
decisions.blockedBy: Needs IdP customer segmentation analysis and expected volume projections to justify tooling investment vs manual process

arguments.for: Scales onboarding capacity, reduces time-to-value for IdP customers, captures process knowledge, enables self-service future
arguments.against: Premature optimization if IdP volume low, internal tooling diverts from customer-facing features, manual process may reveal better requirements

decisions.title: Migration from legacy IngestionConfigFlow proto
decisions.context: ENG-9954 switching data-batcher to legacy proto. Architectural backtrack signal - why reverting? May indicate tech debt in 'Nail the Core' foundation.

arguments.for: Unblock immediate production issues, legacy system more stable, reduces risk during high-velocity period
arguments.against: Accumulates tech debt, may require re-migration later, signals premature abstraction in new proto design

keyResults.name: Explore V2 data loading <3s for 90% of queries

timeAudit.weekSummary: Heavy decision-making week with architectural shifts (client-side parsing, service workers). Meeting load 40% above target. Need 2 focus blocks Friday for SCIM enterprise decision doc.
alerts: 3 architecture decisions due by 4/17 - need dedicated review session
